text: <par>'dead oxen or horses.' probably refers to all draft animals (e.g., water buffalo).</par>
<par>Jianchuan <latinform>ɕi³³ ŋɯ̱̃²¹ ɕi³³ mæ³³</latinform> lit. "dead ox, dead horse". Probably refers to all draft animals (e.g. water buffalo).</par>
<par>'dead tigers or panthers.'probably refers to all wild animals.</par>
<par>Jianchuan <latinform>ɕi³³ lo̱²¹ ɕi³³ pã̱⁴²</latinform> lit. "dead tigers, dead panthers". Probably refers to all wild animals.</par>
<par>'dead dogs or cats.' probably refers to all domestic animals (e.g. chickens).</par>
<par>Jianchuan <latinform>ɕi³³ kʰuã³³ ɕi³³ ʔã⁵⁵ nĩ⁵⁵</latinform> lit. "dead dogs, dead cats". Probably refers to all domestic animals (e.g. chickens).</par>

<par>The last syllable has been tagged 1276 by analogy with the Tujia forms for ‘chest’ <latinform>li⁵⁵ kʰo⁵⁵ tsʰũ⁵⁵</latinform> (43384,127201) and <latinform>li⁵⁵kho⁵⁵tshoŋ⁵⁵</latinform> (193110), since the second syllables of the Bai and Tujia forms seem to match up as well.</par>
<par>See prev. p: morphem.fu;and above. Not b (<latinform>phok</latinform>)?</par>
<par>Loan from Chinese, cf. Chinese gān 肝 'liver'.</par>
